subject: Sports
title: Expected Goals: Football's New Metric
description: A fast breakdown of Expected Goals: Football's New Metric for builders.

hook.text: A striker scores 10 goals from chances worth 15. Is he clinical or unlucky? Expected goals has the answer.

buildup.text: xG assigns a probability to every shot based on location, angle, body part, and defensive pressure.

discovery.text: A penalty has an xG of 0.76. A shot from 30 yards is about 0.03. Not all goals are created equal.

twist.text: xG reveals when teams are lucky or unlucky—winning despite creating few chances, or losing despite domination.

climax.text: Clubs now use xG models to recruit strikers, evaluate defenders, and predict future results.

punchline.text: Not all shots are equal. xG measures what the eye cannot.

quiz.question: What does expected goals (xG) measure?
options: The probability of a shot resulting in a goal based on multiple factors
How many goals a team will score in a season
The speed of a shot on goal
